description_long: This is a medium-sized, open-ended wrench with a visibly weathered, rusty surface, suggesting prolonged exposure to the elements or heavy use. The wrench features a single open jaw at one end, designed for gripping and turning nuts or bolts, and a solid, elongated handle with a circular hole at the opposite end, likely for hanging or storage. The entire tool is constructed from metal, most likely steel, and displays a reddish-brown patina of rust across its surface, giving it a rough, aged appearance. The handle is straight and tapers slightly towards the jaw, with no visible rubber or ergonomic grip enhancements. The open jaw is fixed and not adjustable, with parallel flat surfaces for engaging fasteners. This type of wrench is commonly found in garages, workshops, or urban maintenance settings, and is suitable for manual mechanical tasks.
description: A rusted, open-ended metal wrench with a fixed jaw and a straight handle featuring a hanging hole. The surface is rough and aged, showing significant wear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open jaw and the full length of the handle with the hanging hole clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the thinness of the wrench and the flat, parallel surfaces of the jaw.
description_view_2: End-on view emphasizing the circular hanging hole and the width of the jaw opening.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again showing the length and straightness of the handle and the fixed jaw.
attribute_rust_or_wear_marks: Extensive rust and wear marks are visible across the entire surface, especially on the jaw and handle.
